mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds the 6.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand recovery, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive z-scores of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance, yielding a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 reflects severe supply-side contraction that immediately pressures corporate earnings expectations, triggering negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are non-negative in greater than 60 percent of observations when industrial production drops below the 95.0 index level.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index exceeding 230.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of months following CPIAUCSL readings above 230.0, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates signal a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, indicating no joint predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects robust economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth, leading to positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, with a Spearman rho consistently below 0.05 across out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing its 2017 baseline of 100.0 signals capacity utilization recovery and operational leverage, generating positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of observations when industrial production exceeds the 100.0 baseline threshold.
